theme_notes: Note: web search unavailable today. Taglines draw from the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage (live in USA/Canada/Mexico, day 10), the AI disruption zeitgeist, and US midterm election campaign season.
settled: The balance is level. More than can be said for any World Cup group right now.
All square. Argentina didn't even manage this in their last final.
Zero owed. The most shocking result of the tournament so far.
youOwe: Less than a World Cup stadium hot dog. The humiliation is free.
A rounding error, cosmically. Still, pay it.
Your AI assistant did the math. It sighs every time it opens your profile.
A World Cup fan zone entry fee. For one. You can't even take them.
Enough to buy a campaign bumper sticker and nothing else. Both are embarrassing.
The AI is watching your spending. It filed a report. The tone was concerned.
A World Cup jersey plus a hat plus denial. That's your whole personality right now.
Midterm campaign donation territory. And you're losing in the polls.
The AI put a flag on your account. Not a literal one. More of a warning flag.
Group stage scalper prices for the bad seats. You chose this.
You've crossed from awkward into legally interesting territory.
An AI runs your finances better and it doesn't even have to live with your partner.
That's a World Cup travel package for one. You both know who's traveling alone.
Super-PAC money. Daumi is running against you. The polling is unkind.
The AI flagged this for review. It then asked not to be assigned to your account.
FIFA's legal team has seen numbers like this. They took notes.
You've become a geopolitical situation. Observers have been dispatched.
This debt is old enough to have a Wikipedia page. The citations are embarrassing.
theyOwe: They owe you less than a scarf from the World Cup gift shop. The symbolism stings.
A rounding error on their end. A matter of principle on yours.
Nil-nil in a group game both sides agreed not to try. It's still on record.
Enough for World Cup stadium beer. They drank it. You have the tab.
Not enough to fight over. Not small enough to forget. Perfect resentment calibration.
The AI sees this and feels something. It calls it 'second-hand fiscal embarrassment.'
That's a World Cup official kit. They're wearing yours.
Campaign ad money. The opposition research on them writes itself. File it.
The AI calculated their repayment timeline. It returned an error.
They owe you a World Cup experience, minus the flights, the stadium, and the point.
A grand in. Their excuses are still free, which is somehow worse.
You've become their fiscal policy. You didn't run for that office.
That's FIFA-fine money. You deserve a governing body. Start filing.
Midterm recount territory. Demand a full audit. Bring paper.
The AI calculated their debt-to-income ratio and requested a transfer out of your household.
World Cup prize money. You were eliminated before the tournament even started. By them.
You are their central bank. You did not consent to this role. Set interest rates.
This is a bilateral financial incident. The UN is reviewing whether to intervene.
